extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb won Stage 3 sprint to Sisteron from sixth position with 200m left, timing acceleration perfectly to beat BENNETT Sam.
tactical_patterns: EWAN Caleb deliberately held sixth/seventh position until 200m, demonstrating calculated late acceleration strategy despite headwind risk exposure.
PEREZ Anthony twice outsprinted COSNEFROY Benoît on climbs despite COSNEFROY sitting on his wheel, revealing physical superiority matters when drafting advantage exists.
COSNEFROY Benoît counter-attacked immediately after breakaway return despite teammate COUSIN Jérôme holding 3:40 gap, showing AG2R's tactically confused mountain jersey chase.
Lotto Soudal delivered EWAN to sprint without GILBERT Philippe leadout after losing two riders Stage 1, proving team depth substitution works short-term.

rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: won from extremely far back (6th/7th at 200m) suggesting sprint speed margin allows tactical patience most sprinters cannot risk
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: secured clear sprint lane and second place, indicating positioning discipline superior to pure speed on windy finishes
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: present in sprint finale but commentary notes distance to make up, suggesting positioning rather than legs limited result
rider_signals.NIZZOLO Giacomo: neutral: held perfect dead-center position but no result mentioned, implying final 200m acceleration insufficient despite tactical positioning
rider_signals.TRENTIN Matteo: neutral: mentioned alongside EWAN at finish but no placement given, suggesting close but not podium sprint
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: passed by VALGREN Michael on inside at line after earlier position, showing either fading or poor line choice
rider_signals.PEREZ Anthony: positive: won two categorized climbs against COSNEFROY despite tactical disadvantage, now virtual KOM leader showing climbing strength exceeds sprinter profile
rider_signals.COSNEFROY Benoît: negative: despite sitting on PEREZ wheel twice lost both climbs, then attacked with teammate already clear, revealing poor tactical decision-making
rider_signals.COUSIN Jérôme: neutral: held 3:40 breakaway gap while COSNEFROY attacked from peloton, highlighting team communication breakdown at AG2R
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: retained yellow but commentary questions summit finish ability tomorrow with only seconds buffer, suggesting vulnerability recognized by analysts
